claimText: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) updated its wind turbine approval system by renaming RLMM to the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM) for Wind.
evidenceExcerpt: MNRE updated the wind turbine approval system from RLMM to ALMM (Wind).
examUse: Statement-based question on recent renewable energy policy updates and the nodal ministry.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Power instead of MNRE
ALMM applies exclusively to solar energy

claimText: The ALMM (Wind) framework mandates the sourcing of key wind turbine components from approved Indian suppliers and enforces data localization requirements.
evidenceExcerpt: ALMM Wind mandates sourcing key components from approved Indian suppliers and requires data localization.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific compliance requirements of the ALMM (Wind) policy.
distractorHooks: Mandates technology transfer instead of data localization

claimText: India ranks 4th globally in terms of installed wind power capacity.
evidenceExcerpt: India ranks 4th in the world for installed wind power capacity.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multi-statement question about India's global standing in renewable energy.
distractorHooks: India ranks 4th in solar power but not wind power

claimText: The National Institute of Wind Energy estimates India's wind power potential to be 1164 GW at 150 meters above ground level.
evidenceExcerpt: India's wind potential is estimated at 1164 GW at 150 meters above ground level by the National Institute of Wind Energy.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's renewable energy potential and the assessing agency.
distractorHooks: Potential assessed at 100 meters instead of 150 meters
Assessed by the Central Electricity Authority instead of the National Institute of Wind Energy
Wind potential is estimated at 500 GW
